name: Smithery
required_fields: Server runtime config (command, args, env vars)
notes: Largest MCP marketplace with 100K+ tools and skills. Focused on AI agents. Auto-indexes from npm/pypi/GitHub. Publishes via Smithery CLI or docs submission. Provides install analytics, OAuth support, and managed hosting. High developer traffic from Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other AI agent users.

name: Glama
notes: Claims 37,615+ MCP servers, 50,000+ developers, 1M+ tool calls/month. Superset of official MCP Registry. Auto-discovers from GitHub; maintainer-verified. Scans every server for tool schemas, quality scoring (TDQS), and safety analysis. Includes browser inspector for testing. Strong developer audience from Cursor, Claude Desktop, etc.

name: Pipedream
required_fields: OAuth2 / API key / token auth configuration
npm package name or GitHub repo
notes: Major integration platform (11.5K+ GitHub stars). Connects APIs, AI, databases and more via workflows. Huge developer audience - 200K+ developers. Free tier available. Submissions via GitHub PR to pipedreamhq/pipedream. Strong fit for API services that need workflow automation use cases.

name: MCP.so
notes: Self-described as 'the largest collection of MCP Servers'. Has web form submission for adding servers. Supports multi-language (EN/ZH/JA). Focused on MCP servers for Claude Desktop. Good for discoverability but less traffic than Smithery or Glama based on audience size indicators.

name: Toolbase
notes: SaaS tool discovery platform - more focused on general business software than developer APIs or MCP servers. Lower developer traffic compared to the MCP-specific directories. Better suited for general SaaS listings than specialized API services targeting the MCP ecosystem.
